title: Rapturous Cantonese Bitter Melon Noodles
description: Velvety stir-fried chow fun with bitter melon and a savory enchilada sauce, a romantic twist on classic Cantonese flavor.
instructions: Slice the bitter melon lengthwise, remove the seeds, and cut into thin half-moons, then rinse and pat dry.
Whisk together the enchilada sauce, soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, vinegar, garlic, and ginger in a bowl to create the sauce.
Heat the sesame oil in a large wok over medium-high heat; add the bitter melon and stir-fry until it is slightly softened, about 2 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper to the wok and continue stir-frying for another minute.
Push the vegetables to the side of the wok, pour the sauce over them, swirl to coat, then fold it into the bitter melon.
Add the pre-soaked chow fun to the wok, tossing gently to combine and coat the noodles with the sauce, cooking for 2 to 3 minutes until the noodles are hot and glossy.
Taste the dish and adjust seasoning with extra soy sauce or sugar if needed.
Serve the noodles hot, optionally garnishing with sliced scallions or toasted sesame seeds.
